Project: Dogeaterx Project
Type: FiveM framework core resource (es_extended fork)
Runtime: Lua 5.4 on FXServer / GTA V FiveM

Overview
- This repository is the core framework resource for a FiveM server.
- It provides player lifecycle, accounts, inventory, loadout, jobs, callbacks, commands, metadata, and shared utility behavior.
- It uses oxmysql for persistence and ox_lib for helpers, callbacks, classes, and utility functions.

Entrypoints
- Server entrypoint: `server/init.lua`
- Client entrypoint: `client/init.lua`
- Resource manifest: `fxmanifest.lua`

Main folders
- `server/`: server bootstrap, ORM, player class, services, persistence, commands, and gameplay modules
- `client/`: client bootstrap, player state, UI helpers, streaming, admin helpers, and gameplay modules
- `shared/`: shared functions, module loading, and common helpers
- `settings/`: public configuration such as starter data, admin groups, identifier type, and feature flags
- `db/`: item data and related bootstrap resources
- `nui/`: web UI source/build output for the in-game UI

Player model
- Active players are stored in `ESX.Players`.
- Players are also indexed by identifier in `Core.PlayersByIdentifier`.
- The main player object is `ExtendedPlayer` from `server/classes/player/init.lua`.
- Internal inventory and loadout storage is normalized for fast keyed lookup by item or weapon name.

Persistence
- Player data is saved to SQL tables through the ORM layer in `server/classes/orm/`.
- Important persisted fields include accounts, inventory, job, loadout, metadata, position, identity fields, and skin.
- Save helpers live in `server/functions/services/players/init.lua`.

Important behaviors
- `ESX.GetPlayerFromId(source)` returns the full `ExtendedPlayer` by default.
- `ESX.GetPlayerFromId(source, filters)` can now return a filtered table containing only selected properties and methods.
- `ESX.GetPlayerFromIdentifier(identifier, filters)` supports the same filtered behavior.
- `ESX.GetPlayerFromIdentifiers(identifier, filters)` is an alias to the identifier lookup helper.
- `xPlayer:getInventory(true)` and `xPlayer:getLoadout(true)` return minimal persistence-oriented data.
- `xPlayer:getInventory()` and `xPlayer:getLoadout()` return array-like data intended for compatibility/public consumers.

Key gameplay systems
- Inventory item add/remove/set/carry logic
- Weapon/loadout management, ammo, components, and tint handling
- Job assignment and job-grade loading
- Player join/load/create/drop flows
- Admin and gameplay commands
- Callback and event helpers for client-server interaction

Implementation notes
- Keep backward compatibility in mind for external resources consuming ESX callbacks and player data.
- Avoid exposing internal mutable hash tables directly from public callbacks unless the contract explicitly expects them.
- When changing inventory/loadout behavior, verify save/load flows, client sync events, and admin commands together.
- Configuration flags in `settings/public.lua` affect identifier strategy, hybrid client data shape, starter values, and debug logging.

Current release
- Framework version is defined in `fxmanifest.lua` and mirrored in `README.md`.
